services.title: General Pest Control
services.description: Comprehensive quarterly or monthly pest prevention for spiders, roaches, silverfish, and common invaders.
services.intro: A single treatment won't keep pests out for long. Our recurring general pest control program creates a protective barrier around your home that breaks pest life cycles — keeping the inside clean all year.
features: Interior and exterior treatment at every visit
Perimeter barrier spray around foundation and entry points
Interior crack-and-crevice treatment in key areas
Cobweb removal from eaves and entry areas
Door sweep and exclusion point report
Monthly, bi-monthly, or quarterly plans available
Free re-treatment between scheduled visits if pests return
Pet and child-safe after 30-minute dry time

process.title: Free Inspection
process.desc: We inspect inside and out, identify pest entry points and conducive conditions, and recommend the right plan.

process.title: Initial Treatment
process.desc: We treat the full interior and exterior — perimeter, attic, crawl space, and all interior hot spots.

process.title: Recurring Service
process.desc: We return on your chosen schedule — monthly, bi-monthly, or quarterly — to maintain the barrier.

process.title: Free Re-Treats
process.desc: If pests return between visits, we come back at no charge. That's our guarantee.

faqs.q: Is the treatment safe for my pets and kids?
faqs.a: Yes. Our products are EPA-registered and safe once dry — typically 30 minutes. We'll let you know when it's safe to re-enter and keep children and pets away during application.

faqs.q: How often should I have my home treated?
faqs.a: Quarterly service is standard for most homes. Higher-risk homes or those with previous infestation history benefit from monthly or bi-monthly service.

faqs.q: What if I see pests between scheduled visits?
faqs.a: Call us and we'll come back at no additional charge. Our service includes free re-treatments between scheduled visits.

services.title: Termite Treatment & Prevention
services.description: Termite inspection, liquid barrier treatments, and bait station programs with annual warranties.
services.intro: Termites cause over $5 billion in property damage annually — more than fires, floods, and windstorms combined. We offer both liquid termiticide barrier treatments and the Sentricon bait station system, depending on what your home needs.
features: Full termite inspection of all accessible areas
Liquid termiticide barrier treatment (Termidor or Altriset)
Sentricon Always Active bait station installation
Termite damage report with photographic documentation
Annual renewal warranty with re-inspection
Soil treatment under slabs and at all entry points
Wood treatment of any active infestation areas
WDO (Wood-Destroying Organism) report available for real estate transactions

process.title: Full Termite Inspection
process.desc: We inspect all accessible areas — crawl spaces, garages, attics, and exterior foundation — for evidence of termite activity.

process.title: Treatment Plan
process.desc: We recommend the right approach — liquid barrier, bait stations, or combination — based on your home's construction and infestation level.

process.title: Treatment Day
process.desc: Liquid treatment involves trenching and rodding around the foundation. Bait stations are installed at regular intervals in the soil.

process.title: Annual Warranty
process.desc: We renew your warranty annually with a re-inspection — giving you continuous protection and early detection.

faqs.q: How do I know if I have termites?
faqs.a: Signs include mud tubes on foundation walls, soft or hollow-sounding wood, discarded wings near windows, and visible damage to wood. Many infestations have no obvious signs — which is why annual inspections matter.

faqs.q: What's the difference between liquid treatment and bait stations?
faqs.a: Liquid treatment creates an immediate chemical barrier in the soil. Bait stations work more slowly — termites feed on bait and carry it back to the colony. Both are effective; we recommend based on your situation.

faqs.q: How long does termite treatment last?
faqs.a: Termidor liquid treatment remains active in the soil for 10+ years. Bait stations require annual monitoring. We include an annual renewal warranty with every treatment program.

services.title: Rodent Control
services.description: Exclusion, trapping, and sanitization to eliminate mice and rats and keep them out for good.
services.intro: Rodents don't just damage food — they chew wiring, contaminate insulation, and carry disease. The only lasting solution is exclusion: sealing every entry point. We trap, exclude, and clean so rodents can't return.
features: Full interior and exterior rodent inspection
Identification of all entry points (gaps, pipes, vents, roofline)
Snap trap placement in key interior activity areas
Exterior tamper-resistant bait station installation
Exclusion sealing with copper mesh, hardware cloth, and caulk
Contaminated insulation identification and removal referral
Follow-up visits to check traps and reset as needed
Attic and crawl space inspection included

process.title: Inspection
process.desc: We locate all active areas, entry points, and runways — inside and out — including attic and crawl space.

process.title: Trapping
process.desc: We place traps in all active areas to reduce the population quickly while exclusion work is planned.

process.title: Exclusion
process.desc: Every entry point is sealed with durable materials rodents cannot chew through — copper mesh, caulk, and hardware cloth.

process.title: Follow-Up
process.desc: We return to check traps, confirm exclusion is holding, and monitor for any new activity.

faqs.q: Why do I keep getting mice even after I set traps?
faqs.a: Traps reduce population but don't solve the problem. Mice enter through gaps as small as a dime. Without exclusion — sealing every entry point — new mice will keep coming in.

faqs.q: Is rodenticide safe around my pets?
faqs.a: We use tamper-resistant bait stations that prevent pets and children from accessing the bait. We'll walk you through station placement and any precautions at your appointment.

faqs.q: How long does it take to get rid of a rodent problem?
faqs.a: With trapping and exclusion together, most infestations are resolved in 2–4 weeks. Severe infestations or large entry point counts may take longer.

services.title: Bed Bug Treatment
services.description: Whole-room heat treatment or chemical treatment to eliminate bed bugs at all life stages.
services.intro: Bed bugs are notoriously difficult to eliminate with DIY methods because eggs resist most sprays. We offer whole-room heat treatment — raising room temperatures above 120°F — which kills bed bugs and eggs in a single day.
features: Full bed bug inspection with detection tools
Whole-room heat treatment up to 135°F
Chemical spray follow-up for perimeter protection
Mattress and box spring encasement recommendation
All life stages eliminated including eggs
Same-day or next-day service available
30-day re-treatment warranty
Discreet service vehicles for privacy

process.title: Inspection
process.desc: We inspect all beds, furniture, baseboards, and electrical outlets for live bugs, eggs, and shed skins.

process.title: Heat Setup
process.desc: We bring in commercial heaters and set up monitoring to ensure all areas reach lethal temperature.

process.title: Heat Treatment
process.desc: The room or unit is held above 120°F for 4–6 hours — killing all bugs and eggs without chemicals.

process.title: Chemical Barrier
process.desc: We apply a residual perimeter spray after treatment to catch any bugs that migrated during heat-up.

faqs.q: Does heat treatment really work in one day?
faqs.a: Yes. Heat treatment is the most effective single-day solution available. Temperatures above 120°F kill bed bugs at all life stages, including eggs, which chemical sprays often can't penetrate.

faqs.q: Do I need to throw away my mattress?
faqs.a: In most cases, no. Heat treatment kills all bed bugs in the mattress. We recommend encasing the mattress after treatment as an added precaution.

faqs.q: How did I get bed bugs?
faqs.a: Bed bugs travel on luggage, clothing, used furniture, and through walls in multi-unit buildings. They're not a sign of uncleanliness — they're opportunistic hitchhikers found in hotels, theatres, and public transit.

services.title: Mosquito Control
services.description: Seasonal mosquito barrier treatment to reclaim your yard — misting systems also available.
services.intro: Mosquitoes breed in standing water and rest in vegetation. Our barrier spray coats all leaf surfaces where mosquitoes harbor — killing existing populations and repelling new ones for 21 days between treatments.
features: 21-day barrier spray applied to all vegetation and resting areas
Standing water treatment with BTi larvicide
Identification and recommendation for water source elimination
Seasonal programs (April–October) or one-time event treatment
In2Care station installation for enhanced control
Automated misting system installation available
All products approved for residential use around people and pets
Same-week scheduling during peak season

process.title: Yard Assessment
process.desc: We identify resting areas, water sources, and breeding sites — the key to effective mosquito control.

process.title: Barrier Spray
process.desc: We treat all vegetation, shaded areas, and fence lines where mosquitoes rest during the day.

process.title: Larvicide Treatment
process.desc: We treat any standing water — gutters, birdbaths, low spots — with BTi biological larvicide.

process.title: 21-Day Cycle
process.desc: We return every 21 days through the season to maintain the barrier as plants grow and rains dilute treatment.

faqs.q: How quickly does the treatment work?
faqs.a: You'll notice a significant reduction within 24–48 hours. Full effectiveness builds after the second treatment as we establish consistent coverage.

faqs.q: Is the treatment safe for my kids and pets?
faqs.a: Yes, once dry — typically 30 minutes. We'll notify you when it's safe to re-enter your yard.

faqs.q: What if it rains right after treatment?
faqs.a: Our products bond to leaf surfaces and are rain-fast after about 1 hour. If it rains heavily within the first hour, we'll re-treat at no charge.

services.title: Ant & Roach Treatment
services.description: Targeted ant and cockroach elimination using gel baits, dusts, and perimeter barrier sprays.
services.intro: Ants and cockroaches require different strategies. Ants need bait that workers carry back to kill the colony. Roaches need gel bait in harborage areas plus exclusion of water and food sources. We use the right product for each pest.
features: Species identification to select the right treatment approach
Gel bait placement in cockroach harborage areas
Granular ant bait applied at colony entry points
Perimeter spray to block exterior entry
Borate dust in wall voids and attic for German roaches
Interior crack-and-crevice treatment
Sanitation and conducive condition report
Follow-up visit at 2 weeks to confirm elimination

process.title: Inspection & ID
process.desc: We identify the species — German roach vs. American roach, odorous house ant vs. carpenter ant — because treatment differs by species.

process.title: Targeted Treatment
process.desc: Gel bait, granular bait, spray, or dust — we use the right product in the right place for maximum effectiveness.

process.title: Exclusion Recommendations
process.desc: We identify and note moisture sources, food access points, and entry cracks that invite reinfestation.

process.title: Follow-Up Visit
process.desc: We return at 2 weeks to evaluate results, rebait if needed, and confirm the infestation is resolved.

faqs.q: Why aren't over-the-counter roach sprays working?
faqs.a: Most retail sprays are repellent — they scatter roaches without killing the colony. Professional gel baits are non-repellent and are carried back to hidden harborage areas, eliminating the whole population.

faqs.q: How long does ant or roach treatment take to work?
faqs.a: Gel bait for roaches typically shows dramatic reduction in 5–7 days. Ant bait can take 1–2 weeks as workers carry it back to the colony.

faqs.q: Do I need to leave my home during treatment?
faqs.a: Not for gel bait treatments. If we're doing a spray treatment, we ask that you and your pets stay out for 30 minutes while the product dries.

howItWorks.h2: Four steps from infestation to protected.

steps.title: Free Inspection
steps.desc: A licensed technician inspects your home, identifies pest species and entry points, and recommends a plan.

steps.title: Targeted Treatment
steps.desc: We use the right product for your specific pest — no one-size-fits-all spray routines.

steps.title: Ongoing Protection
steps.desc: Recurring service maintains the protective barrier and catches new pressures before they become infestations.

steps.title: Guaranteed
steps.desc: If pests return between scheduled visits, we come back and re-treat at no additional charge.

serviceOptions: Not Sure — I Need an Inspection

items.q: Are your products safe for children and pets?
items.a: Yes. All products we use are EPA-registered and safe for occupants once dry — typically 30 minutes after application. We'll advise you on any product-specific precautions at your appointment.

items.q: Do I need to be home during the treatment?
items.a: For interior treatment, we ask that an adult be present. For exterior-only service, we can treat without you home if you prefer.

items.q: How long does a treatment take?
items.a: A standard general pest control treatment takes 30–60 minutes for an average home. Specialized treatments like termite barrier or heat treatment take longer and will be explained at booking.
We guarantee your satisfaction. If pests return between scheduled visits, we'll come back and re-treat at no charge. If you're not satisfied after the re-treatment, we'll refund your last service.

items.q: How do I know which plan is right for me?
items.a: Our free inspection determines your pest pressure, entry points, and conducive conditions. We'll recommend the plan that matches your situation — and never upsell services you don't need.

cta.h2: Don't share your home with pests.
cta.subtext: Get a free inspection from a licensed pest professional — same-day service available and all treatments backed by our satisfaction guarantee.
